incorrects.description: Student selected 3 × 5, reversing groups and items per group

params.description: All five circles highlight to emphasize the number of groups.

steps.text: You switched the numbers. The first number is the groups. Count the circles. How many groups do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 + 3, using addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Circles highlight sequentially to show groups.

steps.text: You're adding, not multiplying. When we have equal groups, we multiply. The × symbol shows groups of the same size. Which expression uses ×?

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 + 5, using addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Circles highlight to emphasize the group structure.

steps.text: That's adding. Equal groups need multiplication. The × symbol means groups of the same size. Look at the expressions again. Which one uses ×?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System first highlights all five circles sequentially (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), then zooms to one circle and counts the three dots inside (1, 2, 3).

steps.text: Let me show you. First, count the circles: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Five groups. Now look inside one circle: 1, 2, 3. Three dots in each group. That gives us 5 × 3. The first number is always the groups. The second number is what's in each group. The answer is 5 × 3.
